title: March 4, 2026
summary: Per-ticker trade modes for granular execution control, risk hot-modify correctness fixes, and optimistic UI for instant feedback.

features.title: Per-Ticker Trade Modes
features.summary: Control each ticker independently: Normal, Exit Only, or No Orders mode with instant UI feedback.
features.description:
New per-ticker trade mode system adds granular control over the execution engine. Each ticker can be set to one of three modes:

• **Normal** — full trading, entries and exits allowed
• **Exit Only** — only exit/risk orders allowed, no new entries
• **No Orders** — all automated orders blocked, existing working orders cancelled

Modes are enforced as Gate 0 in the order submission path, before budget checks. NO_ORDERS mode automatically cancels all working orders for the ticker when activated. Modes persist across agent restarts and are visible on the Signals tab with color-coded controls and ticker tab badges.

features.title: Risk Hot-Modify Correctness Fix
features.summary: Fixed broken risk config updates that silently failed when modifying running positions.
features.description:
Fixed a critical bug where updating risk parameters on running positions via the dashboard config panel would silently fail. The risk manager's `update_risk_config()` method referenced an uninitialized `self.config` attribute instead of the correct `_risk_config` snapshot.

Additionally fixed a key mismatch between the dashboard UI (sending `risk_profit_targets_enabled`) and the backend translator (expecting `risk_profit_taking_enabled`). The translator now accepts both keys with a fallback chain.

When disabling risk levels that have TRIGGERED orders in flight, those orders are now properly cancelled via IB.

features.title: Optimistic UI for Trade Mode Changes
features.summary: Instant visual feedback when changing ticker modes, with stale-poll protection.
features.description: Trade mode changes now update the UI instantly without waiting for the server round-trip. A `fresh=1` query parameter bypasses the relay's cached telemetry to confirm the mutation landed. A pending-modes guard prevents stale poll responses from reverting the optimistic state. On error, the UI rolls back to the previous mode automatically.
